input:
# Microcycle — Week of February 23, 2026

**Program:** Joint-Friendly Strength + Muscle (3-Day Healthy Aging Cycle)  
**Phase:** Phase 1 — Foundation & Tissue Tolerance (Week 1 of 4)  
**User:** Tom Anderson  

## Schedule
- **Mon:** Full Body A (Squat emphasis + horizontal press/pull) (YMCA)
- **Tue:** Rest / easy walk + 5–10 min mobility (optional)
- **Wed:** Full Body B (Hinge emphasis + vertical pull + split squat) (YMCA)
- **Thu:** Rest
- **Fri:** Full Body C (Hypertrophy + mobility/athletic emphasis) (YMCA)
- **Sat:** Rest / easy cardio 20–30 min zone-2 (optional)
- **Sun:** Rest

## Week Overview
Week 1 of Phase 1 is about establishing **pain-free “lanes”** for your right shoulder and left knee while rebuilding work capacity. Loads are intentionally conservative (mostly **RPE 6–7**)—you should finish sessions feeling better than when you walked in. This week also functions as a **baseline week**: keep notes on what felt great vs. cranky so we can progress cleanly next week.

---

# Workout — Monday, February 23, 2026
**Focus:** Full Body A — knee-friendly squat + joint-friendly press + upper back  
**Location:** YMCA  
**Duration:** ~75 minutes  

## Warm-Up (12–15 minutes)
1. **Bike (easy):** 4 minutes
2. **Shoulder prep circuit (2 rounds):**
   - Band pull-aparts: × 12
   - Cable face pull (very light): 25 lb × 12
   - Cable external rotation (elbow tucked): 5 lb × 12/side
3. **Knee/hip prep (2 rounds):**
   - Terminal knee extension (band): × 12/side
   - Glute bridge: × 10 (2-sec squeeze)
   - Bodyweight box squat to comfortable depth: × 8

## Main Workout

### 1. Goblet Squat to Box (controlled)
**Target:** 3 × 8 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 30 lb DB × 8 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 45 lb DB × 8 (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 60 lb DB × 8 (RPE 6)
- **Set 4:** 60 lb DB × 8 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 5:** 60 lb DB × 8 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Sit back to the box softly (no “plop”), slight forward torso is fine. Stop 1–2 reps before any knee irritation.

### 2. DB Incline Press (neutral grip if possible)
**Target:** 3 × 10 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 25s × 10 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 35s × 10 (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 45s × 10 (RPE 6)
- **Set 4:** 45s × 10 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 5:** 45s × 10 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Shoulder-friendly setup: shoulder blades “down and back,” elbows ~30–45° from ribs, stop a rep short of any sharp shoulder sensation.

### 3. Chest-Supported Row (machine)
**Target:** 3 × 10 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 70 lb × 10 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 90 lb × 10 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 90 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 90 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** 1-second squeeze at the top; keep neck long, don’t shrug.

### 4. Barbell Romanian Deadlift
**Target:** 2 × 10 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 95 lb × 8 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 115 lb × 8 (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 135 lb × 10 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 4:** 135 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Soft knees, hips back, feel hamstrings load. Stop set if you lose lat tension (bar drifting away).

### 5. Cable Face Pull
**Target:** 2 × 15 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 35 lb × 15 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 2:** 35 lb × 15 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Pull to nose/forehead, rotate thumbs back slightly, no low-back arch.

### 6. Cable External Rotation (easy)
**Target:** 2 × 12/side @ easy (RPE 5–6)  
- **Set 1:** 5 lb × 12/side
- **Set 2:** 5 lb × 12/side
**Rest:** 45–60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** This is “grease the groove,” not a burn-fest.

### 7. Pallof Press (cable)
**Target:** 2 × 10/side @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 20 lb × 10/side (RPE 6)
- **Set 2:** 20 lb × 10/side (RPE 6.5)
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Exhale as you press out; resist rotation—don’t twist.

## Cool Down (6–8 minutes)
1. **Couch stretch (hip flexor):** 45 sec/side
2. **Hamstring stretch (gentle):** 45 sec/side
3. **Doorway pec stretch (low angle):** 45 sec/side
4. **Thoracic extension on bench/roller:** 6 slow breaths

## Notes
- Goal today was **smooth, pain-free reps** and collecting baseline loads.
- If shoulder felt “pinchy” on incline, next week we’ll reduce incline angle or swap to machine press.
- Total time: ~75 minutes (estimated 75).

---

# Workout — Wednesday, February 25, 2026
**Focus:** Full Body B — hinge emphasis + vertical pull + unilateral stability  
**Location:** YMCA  
**Duration:** ~75–85 minutes  

## Warm-Up (12–15 minutes)
1. **Rower (easy):** 4 minutes
2. **Shoulder/scap prep (2 rounds):**
   - Band pull-aparts: × 12
   - Scapular pulldown (straight-arm, light): 30 lb × 12
   - Cable external rotation: 5 lb × 10/side
3. **Hip/knee prep (2 rounds):**
   - Glute bridge: × 10
   - Bodyweight split squat (supported): × 6/side
   - Hip hinge drill with dowel (or empty hands): × 8

## Main Workout

### 1. Trap Bar Deadlift (high handles if available)
**Target:** 4 × 5 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 95 lb × 5 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 135 lb × 5 (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 165 lb × 5 (warm-up)
- **Set 4:** 185 lb × 5 (RPE 6)
- **Set 5:** 185 lb × 5 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 6:** 185 lb × 5 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 7:** 185 lb × 5 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 3 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Brace first (360° breath), then pull. Think “push the floor away.” No grinding reps.

### 2. Neutral-Grip Lat Pulldown
**Target:** 3 × 10 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 80 lb × 10 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 110 lb × 10 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 120 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 120 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** Slight lean back OK; pull elbows to your back pockets. Stop before shoulder discomfort.

### 3. Supported Split Squat (DBs, hold rack for balance)
**Target:** 2 × 8/side @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** Bodyweight × 6/side (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 15s × 8/side (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 25s × 8/side (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 4:** 25s × 8/side (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Keep shin angle comfortable; shorten stance and reduce depth if knee complains. Controlled down, smooth up.

### 4. Half-Kneeling Landmine Press (one arm)
**Target:** 3 × 10/side @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 15 lb (on bar) × 10/side (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 25 lb × 10/side (RPE 6)
- **Set 3:** 25 lb × 10/side (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 4:** 25 lb × 10/side (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 60–90 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Press “up and forward.” Ribcage down, glute tight on kneeling side—keep it shoulder-friendly.

### 5. Seated Leg Curl
**Target:** 3 × 12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 50 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 70 lb × 12 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 80 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 80 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 60–90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** 1-second squeeze at peak contraction; don’t let hips pop up.

### 6. Farmer Carry (DBs)
**Target:** 3 × 35 sec (moderate, crisp posture)  
- **Set 1:** 50s × 35 sec
- **Set 2:** 50s × 35 sec
- **Set 3:** 50s × 35 sec
**Rest:** 60–90 seconds between carries  
**Notes:** Tall posture, slow controlled steps, ribs stacked over pelvis.

## Cool Down (6–8 minutes)
1. **Lat stretch (bench-supported):** 45 sec/side
2. **Figure-4 glute stretch:** 45 sec/side
3. **Calf stretch (wall):** 45 sec/side
4. **Easy nasal breathing walk:** 2 minutes

## Notes
- Hinge day should feel like **strong work without back fatigue hangover**.
- If trap bar felt too easy at RPE ≤6 across the board, we’ll add **+10 lb** next week.
- Total time: ~80 minutes (estimated 80).

---

# Workout — Friday, February 27, 2026
**Focus:** Full Body C — hypertrophy + joint-friendly pump + mobility finish  
**Location:** YMCA  
**Duration:** ~70–80 minutes  

## Warm-Up (12–15 minutes)
1. **Bike (easy):** 4 minutes
2. **Shoulder prep (2 rounds):**
   - Band pull-aparts: × 12
   - Cable face pull (light): 25 lb × 12
   - Scap push-ups (hands on bench): × 10
3. **Knee/hip prep (2 rounds):**
   - TKE (band): × 12/side
   - Bodyweight leg press pattern drill (slow partial squats holding rack): × 8
   - Glute bridge: × 10

## Main Workout

### 1. Leg Press (knee-friendly stance)
**Target:** 3 × 12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 180 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 230 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 270 lb × 12 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 4:** 270 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
- **Set 5:** 270 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Feet a bit higher/wider if that keeps knees happier. Depth only as tolerated—no forced deep flexion.

### 2. Seated Cable Row (different handle than Wednesday if possible)
**Target:** 3 × 12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 80 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 110 lb × 12 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 120 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 120 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** No torso swing. Think “elbows back,” pause 1 count.

### 3. Machine Chest Press (neutral-ish grip if available)
**Target:** 3 × 12 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 50 lb/side × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 70 lb/side × 12 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 70 lb/side × 12 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 70 lb/side × 12 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** Keep shoulder blades set; stop a hair short of the deepest stretch if shoulder feels “caught.”

### 4. Hip Thrust (barbell)
**Target:** 3 × 10 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 95 lb × 10 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 135 lb × 10 (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 185 lb × 10 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 4:** 185 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 5:** 185 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Chin tucked, ribs down, full lockout with glutes (not low back). Pause 1 sec at the top.

### 5. Cable Lateral Raise (scapular plane)
**Target:** 2 × 15/side @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 10 lb × 15/side (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 2:** 10 lb × 15/side (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Slight forward angle (thumb slightly up). Stop if you feel shoulder joint pinch.

### 6. Rear Delt Fly (machine)
**Target:** 2 × 15 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 50 lb × 15 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 2:** 50 lb × 15 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Lead with elbows, not hands. No shrugging.

### 7. Standing Calf Raise (machine)
**Target:** 2 × 12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 120 lb × 12 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 2:** 120 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Full stretch at bottom, 1-sec squeeze at top.

### 8. Dead Bug (slow)
**Target:** 2 × 8/side (controlled)  
- **Set 1:** 8/side
- **Set 2:** 8/side
**Rest:** 45–60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Low back stays gently “glued” to the floor—exhale to lock ribs down.

## Cool Down (8–10 minutes)
1. **Hip flexor stretch:** 45 sec/side
2. **Hamstring stretch:** 45 sec/side
3. **Pec minor opener (corner stretch):** 45 sec/side
4. **Ankle rocks (knee-to-wall):** 10/side
5. **Easy breathing (supine):** 6 slow breaths

## Notes
- Today is the “pump + joints feel good” day—leave 1–2 reps in the tank throughout.
- If knees feel loaded after leg press, next week we’ll adjust stance and/or reduce ROM slightly.
- Total time: ~75 minutes (estimated 75).

---

## Weekly Summary
- **Full Body A:** Established baseline loads with goblet squat **60×8×3** and DB incline **45s×10×3** at controlled RPE 6–7.
- **Full Body B:** Trap bar deadlift baseline **185×5×4** (smooth, submax) + solid back work with pulldown **120×10×2**.
- **Full Body C:** Hypertrophy work landed well: leg press **270×12×3** and hip thrust **185×10×3**—good stimulus without joint irritation.
- **Constraint monitoring:** Shoulder managed via neutral-grip incline/machine press and landmine pressing; knee managed via box target, controlled split squats, and conservative leg press depth.
- **Weak points (longevity focus):** Scap/upper-back volume and trunk anti-rotation are in place; keep prioritizing slow eccentrics and clean bracing.
- **Next week:** If RPE stayed ≤7 and joints felt normal the next day, add small progressions: **+5–10 lb** on trap bar and leg press, **+5 lb total** on hip thrust, and/or **+1 rep per set** on presses/rows (whichever keeps you in the RPE 6–7 lane).
context:
<Profile>### IDENTITY
- **Name:** Tom Anderson  
- **Age:** 51  
- **Gender:** Male  
- **Experience level:** **Intermediate** — former college basketball player; lifted on/off ~20 years; **consistent last 3 years** with a sustainable, healthy-aging focus.

---

### GOALS
- **Primary goal(s):**
  - **Build and maintain strength and muscle mass** for healthy aging (no fixed timeline).
- **Secondary goals:**
  - **Joint health** (especially shoulder/knee friendly training)
  - **Mobility** and staying athletic/functional long-term
  - Emphasize **sustainable progression** over maxing out
- **Target events/deadlines:** None specified.

---

## TRAINING CONTEXT

### Schedule & Availability
- **Available training days:** **3 days/week** (specific days not provided)
- **Session duration preferences/constraints:** Not specified
- **Time windows:** Not specified
- **Blocked days with reasons:** Not specified

### Equipment & Environment
- **Primary location:** Commercial gym (YMCA)
- **Available equipment:**
  - Barbells/plates (standard commercial setup)
  - Dumbbells
  - Machines
  - Cables

### Constraints
- **[ACTIVE]** Right shoulder arthritis — **avoid heavy overhead pressing**, prioritize joint-friendly pressing angles and longer warm-ups (**since 2026-02-27**). Monitor symptoms and adjust ROM/load accordingly.
- **[ACTIVE]** History of left knee meniscus surgery — manage knee volume/loading; prioritize controlled ROM and exercise selection as needed (**since 2026-02-27**).
- **[ACTIVE]** Training approach constraint: avoids **max-effort lifts**; benefits from **longer warm-up** and submaximal progression (**since 2026-02-27**).

### Preferences
- **Training style:** Sustainable, joint-friendly strength + hypertrophy; not maxing out
- **Movement preferences:** Avoids heavy overhead pressing; longer warm-up preferred/required
- **Focus areas:** Mobility, joint health, athleticism alongside strength/muscle

---

## METRICS
*(No performance/body comp metrics provided yet — tracking can be added once baseline numbers are shared.)*

**Strength (priority: build/maintain strength + muscle)**
- Bench Press: — (as of 2026-02-27)
- Squat / knee-friendly primary squat pattern: — (as of 2026-02-27)
- Deadlift / hinge pattern: — (as of 2026-02-27)
- Row / pull (machine, DB, cable, or bar): — (as of 2026-02-27)
- Pull-ups or Lat Pulldown: — (as of 2026-02-27)

**Movement Quality**
- Overhead pressing tolerance (pain-free ROM/loading): — (as of 2026-02-27)
- Knee tolerance for squat/lunge patterns: — (as of 2026-02-27)

**Body Composition**
- Body weight: — (as of 2026-02-27)
- Waist or body fat % (optional): — (as of 2026-02-27)

**Endurance**
- Not specified / not currently tracked (as of 2026-02-27)

---

## LOG

## 2026-02-27 — Profile created/updated (intake)
- Set primary goal: build/maintain strength and muscle for healthy aging; secondary focus on joint health, mobility, athleticism.
- Confirmed training frequency target: **3x/week** at **commercial gym (YMCA)** with full standard equipment.
- Documented constraints: **right shoulder arthritis**, **left knee meniscus surgery history**, **needs longer warm-up**, **avoids heavy overhead pressing and max-effort lifting**.
- Metrics not yet provided; placeholders created for baseline collection.

--- 

If you share any current baseline numbers (e.g., recent working weights/reps for bench, a knee-friendly squat pattern, hinge, row/pulldown, plus body weight), I’ll plug them into **METRICS** with dates and start a proper progression history.</Profile>

<Plan>## Header
- **Program name:** Joint-Friendly Strength + Muscle (3-Day Healthy Aging Cycle)
- **Program Owner:** GymText
- **User:** Tom Anderson
- **Goal:** Build and maintain strength and muscle mass for healthy aging (with joint health, mobility, and athleticism)
- **Duration:** **Ongoing with phase cycling**

---

## Program Philosophy
Tom is an intermediate, long-term lifter whose priorities are strength and muscle with a sustainable, joint-friendly approach—especially respecting right shoulder arthritis (avoid heavy overhead pressing) and a left knee meniscus history (manage knee loading and ROM). A **3-day full-body split** maximizes weekly stimulus while keeping per-session fatigue manageable and recovery high. The program uses **submaximal training (mostly RPE 6–8)**, joint-friendly exercise choices (e.g., landmine/incline pressing, controlled squat patterns, trap bar/RDL hinges), and built-in mobility/prehab so training supports longevity. Progression is primarily **rep-then-load (double progression)** with periodic deloads and planned phase shifts so Tom can build for years without needing max-effort lifts.

---

# Phase 1: Foundation & Tissue Tolerance (Weeks 1–4)
Goal: re-groove technique, build work capacity, and establish pain-free/loading “lanes” for shoulder + knee.

### Weekly Pattern (3 days/week)
**Day 1 — Full Body A (Squat emphasis + horizontal press/pull)**
- **Focus:** Knee-friendly squat pattern, joint-friendly pressing, upper back
- **Volume:** ~16–22 working sets
- **Knee-Dominant (knee-friendly):**  
  - **Safety Bar Squat to box** *or* **Goblet Squat**: 3–4 × 6–10 @ **RPE 6–7**  
  *Controlled ROM/tempo builds tolerance without aggravating the meniscus.*
- **Horizontal Press (shoulder-friendly):**  
  - **DB Incline Press (neutral grip if possible)**: 3–4 × 8–12 @ **RPE 6–7**  
  *Incline + DBs typically reduce shoulder irritation vs heavy flat barbell or overhead work.*
- **Horizontal Pull:**  
  - **Chest-supported row (machine/DB)**: 3–4 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7**
- **Hip Hinge (light-moderate):**  
  - **Romanian Deadlift (DB or barbell)**: 2–3 × 8–10 @ **RPE 6–7**
- **Shoulder health / Scap control:**  
  - **Cable face pull**: 2 × 12–15 @ RPE 7  
  - **Cable external rotation**: 1–2 × 12–15 easy
- **Core (anti-rotation):**  
  - **Pallof press**: 2 × 10–12/side

**Day 2 — Full Body B (Hinge emphasis + vertical pull + split squat)**
- **Focus:** Posterior chain strength, back, unilateral leg stability
- **Volume:** ~16–22 working sets
- **Hinge (primary):**  
  - **Trap Bar Deadlift (high handles if needed)**: 3–5 × 4–6 @ **RPE 6–7**  
  *More joint-friendly for many lifters vs straight-bar deadlift; easier to keep positions clean.*
- **Vertical Pull:**  
  - **Lat pulldown (neutral/parallel grip preferred)**: 3–4 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7**
- **Single-Leg (knee-managed):**  
  - **Rear-foot-elevated split squat** *or* **supported split squat**: 2–3 × 8–10/side @ **RPE 6–7**  
  *Unilateral work builds knee resilience and athleticism with controllable loading.*
- **Pressing (very shoulder-friendly angle):**  
  - **Landmine press (half-kneeling)**: 3 × 8–12/side @ **RPE 6–7**  
  *“Up and forward” angle usually tolerates arthritis better than true overhead.*
- **Hamstring / knee support:**  
  - **Seated or lying leg curl**: 2–3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7**
- **Carries (athletic/core):**  
  - **Farmer carry**: 3 × 30–45 sec (moderate, braced, crisp posture)

**Day 3 — Full Body C (Hypertrophy + mobility/athletic emphasis)**
- **Focus:** Higher-rep muscle work, joint-friendly pump, movement quality
- **Volume:** ~18–26 working sets
- **Knee-Dominant (machine-friendly option):**  
  - **Leg press (feet position that’s knee-friendly)**: 3–4 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7**  
  *Stable pattern to build legs without demanding deep knee flexion if not tolerated.*
- **Horizontal Pull (different angle):**  
  - **Cable row**: 3–4 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7**
- **Horizontal Press (variation):**  
  - **Machine chest press** *or* **push-up on handles**: 3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 6–7**
- **Glute-focused hinge:**  
  - **Hip thrust (machine or barbell)**: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7**
- **Delts/upper back (shoulder-friendly):**  
  - **Cable lateral raise (scapular plane)**: 2–3 × 12–20 @ **RPE 7**  
  - **Rear delt fly (machine/cable)**: 2 × 12–20 @ **RPE 7**
- **Calves (optional but helpful for durability):**  
  - **Standing or seated calf raise**: 2–3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7**
- **Trunk:**  
  - **Dead bug**: 2 × 6–10/side (slow, controlled)

### Progression Strategy (Phase 1)
- **Primary method:** **Double progression**
  - Keep load the same until you hit the **top of the rep range on all sets** at the target RPE, then add load next week (typically **+2.5–10 lb** depending on lift).
- **RPE guardrails:** Stay mostly **RPE 6–7**, occasional **RPE 8** on final set of the day if joints feel great.
- **Deload (Week 4):**
  - Reduce **sets by ~40–50%**, keep loads similar but cap at **RPE 6**.
- **Transition to Phase 2:** After Week 4 deload, if shoulder/knee symptoms are stable and loads/reps are progressing.

---

# Phase 2: Strength + Hypertrophy Build (Weeks 5–8)
Goal: keep joints happy while pushing heavier (still submaximal) work on the main lifts and maintaining hypertrophy volume.

### Weekly Pattern (3 days/week)
**Day 1 — Strength A (Squat + Press + Row)**
- **Focus:** Lower strength + upper push/pull strength
- **Volume:** ~14–20 working sets
- **Primary squat pattern:**  
  - **Safety Bar Squat to box** *or* **Front squat to comfortable depth**: 4 × 4–6 @ **RPE 7–8**
- **Primary press (shoulder-friendly):**  
  - **DB incline press**: 4 × 5–8 @ **RPE 7–8**
- **Primary row:**  
  - **Chest-supported row**: 4 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**
- **Accessory posterior chain:**  
  - **Back extension** *or* **RDL**: 2–3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7**
- **Shoulder health:**  
  - **Face pulls**: 2 × 12–15

**Day 2 — Strength B (Hinge + Vertical Pull + Single-leg)**
- **Focus:** Hinge strength, lats/upper back, knee-resilient unilateral work
- **Volume:** ~14–20 working sets
- **Primary hinge:**  
  - **Trap bar deadlift**: 5 × 3–5 @ **RPE 7–8**
- **Vertical pull:**  
  - **Neutral-grip pulldown** *or* **assisted pull-up**: 4 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**
- **Single-leg:**  
  - **Split squat (supported if needed)**: 3 × 6–10/side @ **RPE 7**
- **Press (light/moderate):**  
  - **Half-kneeling landmine press**: 3 × 8–10/side @ **RPE 7**
- **Hamstrings:**  
  - **Leg curl**: 2 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7**

**Day 3 — Hypertrophy C (Volume + athletic accessories)**
- **Focus:** Muscle-building, joint-friendly volume, “stay athletic”
- **Volume:** ~18–26 working sets
- **Leg press** *or* **hack squat (if knee tolerates)**: 3–4 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7–8**
- **Cable row (different grip than Day 1):** 3–4 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7–8**
- **Machine chest press** *or* **push-up**: 3–4 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7–8**
- **Hip thrust:** 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7–8**
- **Lateral raise + rear delt superset:**  
  - Lateral raise: 2–3 × 12–20 @ RPE 7  
  - Rear delt fly: 2–3 × 12–20 @ RPE 7
- **Carry or sled (if available):**  
  - **Farmer carry** *or* **sled push**: 6–10 minutes total, moderate effort  
  *Low-skill conditioning that’s often knee-friendly when dosed conservatively.*

### Progression Strategy (Phase 2)
- **Main lifts (first 1–2 movements/day):**
  - Week-to-week: add **1 rep per set** until top of range, then add load.
  - Keep most work **RPE 7–8**; avoid grinders.
- **Accessories:**
  - Add reps first (e.g., 10 → 15), then add load.
- **Deload (Week 8):**
  - Reduce sets by **~40–60%**, keep technique crisp, cap at **RPE 6–7**.
- **Transition:** If joints feel beat up, repeat Phase 1; if stable and motivated, continue to Phase 3.

---

# Phase 3: Resilient Performance (Weeks 9–12)
Goal: maintain strength while improving joint robustness, mobility, and athletic capacity; slightly more variation to reduce overuse.

### Weekly Pattern (3 days/week)
**Day 1 — Moderate Strength + Tempo**
- **Focus:** Controlled strength (joint-friendly tempo), upper back
- **Volume:** ~14–20 working sets
- **Tempo squat pattern (3-sec down):**  
  - **Goblet squat** *or* **SSB squat to box**: 3–4 × 6–8 @ **RPE 7**  
  *Tempo provides stimulus with lighter load—often friendlier to knees/hips.*
- **DB incline press:** 3–4 × 8–10 @ **RPE 7**
- **Chest-supported row:** 4 × 8–10 @ **RPE 7–8**
- **RDL:** 2–3 × 8–10 @ **RPE 7**
- **Rotator cuff + scap:** 3–5 total sets (face pulls/external rotations)

**Day 2 — Hinge + Power-lite + Pull**
- **Focus:** Hinge strength + athletic intent without maxing out
- **Volume:** ~14–20 working sets
- **Trap bar deadlift (submax):** 4 × 4 @ **RPE 7**
- **Med ball slam** *or* **cable high-to-low chop (power intent):** 4 × 5–8 (fast, crisp)  
  *Keeps athletic qualities without shoulder-hostile overhead loading.*
- **Neutral-grip pulldown:** 4 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7–8**
- **Step-up (low/moderate box):** 2–3 × 8–10/side @ **RPE 7**  
  *Often knee-friendly; adjust height to stay pain-free.*
- **Carry:** 3 × 30–45 sec

**Day 3 — Hypertrophy + Mobility Finish**
- **Focus:** Higher-rep pump + extended mobility
- **Volume:** ~18–26 working sets
- **Leg press:** 3–4 × 12–20 @ **RPE 7**
- **Cable row:** 3–4 × 12–20 @ **RPE 7**
- **Machine chest press / push-ups:** 3–4 × 12–20 @ **RPE 7**
- **Hip thrust or glute bridge:** 3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7**
- **Arms (optional):**  
  - Cable curl: 2 × 10–15  
  - Rope pressdown (pain-free): 2 × 10–15
- **Mobility finisher (8–12 min):**  
  - Thoracic extension + pec minor opener + hip flexor + ankle rocks (choose 3–4 drills)

### Progression Strategy (Phase 3)
- Keep loads mostly stable; progress by **better reps**, slightly more reps, or small load jumps while staying **RPE 6–8**.
- Use this phase as a “consolidation” block: you should finish feeling **better** than when you started.
- **Deload (Week 12):** reduce sets by ~50%, keep movement quality high.

---

## Phase Cycling
- **Default cycle:** **Phase 1 → Phase 2 → Phase 3 → repeat**
- **Typical timing:** 12-week macrocycle with deloads in Weeks **4, 8, 12**
- **How to choose the next phase:**
  - If shoulder/knee irritation rises above “background”: repeat **Phase 1** (rebuild tolerance).
  - If you feel great and want more “strength focus” without maxing: spend extra time in **Phase 2** (extend 4 more weeks before Phase 3).
  - If life stress/sleep is poor: move to **Phase 3** early (consolidate and recover).
- **Long-term progression rule:** You should be able to train year-round without flare-ups; PRs are welcome but **never required**.

---

## (Built-In) Warm-up & Joint Prep Template (10–15 minutes)
Use before every session; take longer on shoulder/knee “stiff” days.
1. **General heat (3–5 min):** bike/rower/fast walk
2. **Shoulder prep (3–4 min):** band pull-aparts + face pulls + light external rotations
3. **Knee/hip prep (3–4 min):** terminal knee extensions (band) + glute bridges + bodyweight squats to comfortable depth
4. **Ramp-up sets:** 3–6 progressively heavier warm-up sets for the first main lift

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27:** Program created based on intake constraints (right shoulder arthritis, left knee meniscus history) and preference for submaximal progression with longer warm-ups.</Plan>

<Week># Microcycle — Week of February 23, 2026

**Program:** Joint-Friendly Strength + Muscle (3-Day Healthy Aging Cycle)  
**Phase:** Phase 1 — Foundation & Tissue Tolerance (Week 1 of 4)  
**User:** Tom Anderson  

## Schedule
- **Mon:** Full Body A (Squat emphasis + horizontal press/pull) (YMCA)
- **Tue:** Rest / easy walk + 5–10 min mobility (optional)
- **Wed:** Full Body B (Hinge emphasis + vertical pull + split squat) (YMCA)
- **Thu:** Rest
- **Fri:** Full Body C (Hypertrophy + mobility/athletic emphasis) (YMCA)
- **Sat:** Rest / easy cardio 20–30 min zone-2 (optional)
- **Sun:** Rest

## Week Overview
Week 1 of Phase 1 is about establishing **pain-free “lanes”** for your right shoulder and left knee while rebuilding work capacity. Loads are intentionally conservative (mostly **RPE 6–7**)—you should finish sessions feeling better than when you walked in. This week also functions as a **baseline week**: keep notes on what felt great vs. cranky so we can progress cleanly next week.

---

# Workout — Monday, February 23, 2026
**Focus:** Full Body A — knee-friendly squat + joint-friendly press + upper back  
**Location:** YMCA  
**Duration:** ~75 minutes  

## Warm-Up (12–15 minutes)
1. **Bike (easy):** 4 minutes
2. **Shoulder prep circuit (2 rounds):**
   - Band pull-aparts: × 12
   - Cable face pull (very light): 25 lb × 12
   - Cable external rotation (elbow tucked): 5 lb × 12/side
3. **Knee/hip prep (2 rounds):**
   - Terminal knee extension (band): × 12/side
   - Glute bridge: × 10 (2-sec squeeze)
   - Bodyweight box squat to comfortable depth: × 8

## Main Workout

### 1. Goblet Squat to Box (controlled)
**Target:** 3 × 8 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 30 lb DB × 8 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 45 lb DB × 8 (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 60 lb DB × 8 (RPE 6)
- **Set 4:** 60 lb DB × 8 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 5:** 60 lb DB × 8 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Sit back to the box softly (no “plop”), slight forward torso is fine. Stop 1–2 reps before any knee irritation.

### 2. DB Incline Press (neutral grip if possible)
**Target:** 3 × 10 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 25s × 10 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 35s × 10 (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 45s × 10 (RPE 6)
- **Set 4:** 45s × 10 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 5:** 45s × 10 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Shoulder-friendly setup: shoulder blades “down and back,” elbows ~30–45° from ribs, stop a rep short of any sharp shoulder sensation.

### 3. Chest-Supported Row (machine)
**Target:** 3 × 10 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 70 lb × 10 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 90 lb × 10 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 90 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 90 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** 1-second squeeze at the top; keep neck long, don’t shrug.

### 4. Barbell Romanian Deadlift
**Target:** 2 × 10 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 95 lb × 8 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 115 lb × 8 (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 135 lb × 10 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 4:** 135 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Soft knees, hips back, feel hamstrings load. Stop set if you lose lat tension (bar drifting away).

### 5. Cable Face Pull
**Target:** 2 × 15 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 35 lb × 15 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 2:** 35 lb × 15 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Pull to nose/forehead, rotate thumbs back slightly, no low-back arch.

### 6. Cable External Rotation (easy)
**Target:** 2 × 12/side @ easy (RPE 5–6)  
- **Set 1:** 5 lb × 12/side
- **Set 2:** 5 lb × 12/side
**Rest:** 45–60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** This is “grease the groove,” not a burn-fest.

### 7. Pallof Press (cable)
**Target:** 2 × 10/side @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 20 lb × 10/side (RPE 6)
- **Set 2:** 20 lb × 10/side (RPE 6.5)
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Exhale as you press out; resist rotation—don’t twist.

## Cool Down (6–8 minutes)
1. **Couch stretch (hip flexor):** 45 sec/side
2. **Hamstring stretch (gentle):** 45 sec/side
3. **Doorway pec stretch (low angle):** 45 sec/side
4. **Thoracic extension on bench/roller:** 6 slow breaths

## Notes
- Goal today was **smooth, pain-free reps** and collecting baseline loads.
- If shoulder felt “pinchy” on incline, next week we’ll reduce incline angle or swap to machine press.
- Total time: ~75 minutes (estimated 75).

---

# Workout — Wednesday, February 25, 2026
**Focus:** Full Body B — hinge emphasis + vertical pull + unilateral stability  
**Location:** YMCA  
**Duration:** ~75–85 minutes  

## Warm-Up (12–15 minutes)
1. **Rower (easy):** 4 minutes
2. **Shoulder/scap prep (2 rounds):**
   - Band pull-aparts: × 12
   - Scapular pulldown (straight-arm, light): 30 lb × 12
   - Cable external rotation: 5 lb × 10/side
3. **Hip/knee prep (2 rounds):**
   - Glute bridge: × 10
   - Bodyweight split squat (supported): × 6/side
   - Hip hinge drill with dowel (or empty hands): × 8

## Main Workout

### 1. Trap Bar Deadlift (high handles if available)
**Target:** 4 × 5 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 95 lb × 5 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 135 lb × 5 (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 165 lb × 5 (warm-up)
- **Set 4:** 185 lb × 5 (RPE 6)
- **Set 5:** 185 lb × 5 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 6:** 185 lb × 5 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 7:** 185 lb × 5 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 3 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Brace first (360° breath), then pull. Think “push the floor away.” No grinding reps.

### 2. Neutral-Grip Lat Pulldown
**Target:** 3 × 10 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 80 lb × 10 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 110 lb × 10 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 120 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 120 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** Slight lean back OK; pull elbows to your back pockets. Stop before shoulder discomfort.

### 3. Supported Split Squat (DBs, hold rack for balance)
**Target:** 2 × 8/side @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** Bodyweight × 6/side (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 15s × 8/side (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 25s × 8/side (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 4:** 25s × 8/side (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Keep shin angle comfortable; shorten stance and reduce depth if knee complains. Controlled down, smooth up.

### 4. Half-Kneeling Landmine Press (one arm)
**Target:** 3 × 10/side @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 15 lb (on bar) × 10/side (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 25 lb × 10/side (RPE 6)
- **Set 3:** 25 lb × 10/side (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 4:** 25 lb × 10/side (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 60–90 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Press “up and forward.” Ribcage down, glute tight on kneeling side—keep it shoulder-friendly.

### 5. Seated Leg Curl
**Target:** 3 × 12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 50 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 70 lb × 12 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 80 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 80 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 60–90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** 1-second squeeze at peak contraction; don’t let hips pop up.

### 6. Farmer Carry (DBs)
**Target:** 3 × 35 sec (moderate, crisp posture)  
- **Set 1:** 50s × 35 sec
- **Set 2:** 50s × 35 sec
- **Set 3:** 50s × 35 sec
**Rest:** 60–90 seconds between carries  
**Notes:** Tall posture, slow controlled steps, ribs stacked over pelvis.

## Cool Down (6–8 minutes)
1. **Lat stretch (bench-supported):** 45 sec/side
2. **Figure-4 glute stretch:** 45 sec/side
3. **Calf stretch (wall):** 45 sec/side
4. **Easy nasal breathing walk:** 2 minutes

## Notes
- Hinge day should feel like **strong work without back fatigue hangover**.
- If trap bar felt too easy at RPE ≤6 across the board, we’ll add **+10 lb** next week.
- Total time: ~80 minutes (estimated 80).

---

# Workout — Friday, February 27, 2026
**Focus:** Full Body C — hypertrophy + joint-friendly pump + mobility finish  
**Location:** YMCA  
**Duration:** ~70–80 minutes  

## Warm-Up (12–15 minutes)
1. **Bike (easy):** 4 minutes
2. **Shoulder prep (2 rounds):**
   - Band pull-aparts: × 12
   - Cable face pull (light): 25 lb × 12
   - Scap push-ups (hands on bench): × 10
3. **Knee/hip prep (2 rounds):**
   - TKE (band): × 12/side
   - Bodyweight leg press pattern drill (slow partial squats holding rack): × 8
   - Glute bridge: × 10

## Main Workout

### 1. Leg Press (knee-friendly stance)
**Target:** 3 × 12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 180 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 230 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 270 lb × 12 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 4:** 270 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
- **Set 5:** 270 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Feet a bit higher/wider if that keeps knees happier. Depth only as tolerated—no forced deep flexion.

### 2. Seated Cable Row (different handle than Wednesday if possible)
**Target:** 3 × 12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 80 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 110 lb × 12 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 120 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 120 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** No torso swing. Think “elbows back,” pause 1 count.

### 3. Machine Chest Press (neutral-ish grip if available)
**Target:** 3 × 12 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 50 lb/side × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 70 lb/side × 12 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 70 lb/side × 12 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 70 lb/side × 12 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** Keep shoulder blades set; stop a hair short of the deepest stretch if shoulder feels “caught.”

### 4. Hip Thrust (barbell)
**Target:** 3 × 10 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 95 lb × 10 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 135 lb × 10 (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 185 lb × 10 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 4:** 185 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 5:** 185 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Chin tucked, ribs down, full lockout with glutes (not low back). Pause 1 sec at the top.

### 5. Cable Lateral Raise (scapular plane)
**Target:** 2 × 15/side @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 10 lb × 15/side (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 2:** 10 lb × 15/side (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Slight forward angle (thumb slightly up). Stop if you feel shoulder joint pinch.

### 6. Rear Delt Fly (machine)
**Target:** 2 × 15 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 50 lb × 15 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 2:** 50 lb × 15 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Lead with elbows, not hands. No shrugging.

### 7. Standing Calf Raise (machine)
**Target:** 2 × 12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 120 lb × 12 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 2:** 120 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Full stretch at bottom, 1-sec squeeze at top.

### 8. Dead Bug (slow)
**Target:** 2 × 8/side (controlled)  
- **Set 1:** 8/side
- **Set 2:** 8/side
**Rest:** 45–60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Low back stays gently “glued” to the floor—exhale to lock ribs down.

## Cool Down (8–10 minutes)
1. **Hip flexor stretch:** 45 sec/side
2. **Hamstring stretch:** 45 sec/side
3. **Pec minor opener (corner stretch):** 45 sec/side
4. **Ankle rocks (knee-to-wall):** 10/side
5. **Easy breathing (supine):** 6 slow breaths

## Notes
- Today is the “pump + joints feel good” day—leave 1–2 reps in the tank throughout.
- If knees feel loaded after leg press, next week we’ll adjust stance and/or reduce ROM slightly.
- Total time: ~75 minutes (estimated 75).

---

## Weekly Summary
- **Full Body A:** Established baseline loads with goblet squat **60×8×3** and DB incline **45s×10×3** at controlled RPE 6–7.
- **Full Body B:** Trap bar deadlift baseline **185×5×4** (smooth, submax) + solid back work with pulldown **120×10×2**.
- **Full Body C:** Hypertrophy work landed well: leg press **270×12×3** and hip thrust **185×10×3**—good stimulus without joint irritation.
- **Constraint monitoring:** Shoulder managed via neutral-grip incline/machine press and landmine pressing; knee managed via box target, controlled split squats, and conservative leg press depth.
- **Weak points (longevity focus):** Scap/upper-back volume and trunk anti-rotation are in place; keep prioritizing slow eccentrics and clean bracing.
- **Next week:** If RPE stayed ≤7 and joints felt normal the next day, add small progressions: **+5–10 lb** on trap bar and leg press, **+5 lb total** on hip thrust, and/or **+1 rep per set** on presses/rows (whichever keeps you in the RPE 6–7 lane).</Week>

## Week 1 Dossier — Feb 23–Mar 1, 2026  
**Program:** Joint-Friendly Strength + Muscle (3-Day Healthy Aging Cycle)  
**Phase:** **Phase 1 — Foundation & Tissue Tolerance** (Week **1/4**)  
**Athlete:** Tom Anderson (51)  
**Theme / Focus:** Establish **pain-free loading lanes** (right shoulder / left knee), rebuild work capacity, and capture baseline working loads.  
**Intensity Guardrails:** Mostly **RPE 6–7** (leave ~1–3 reps in reserve; no grinders)  
**Locations:** YMCA (commercial gym)

---

## Weekly Schedule
- **Mon (2/23):** Full Body A — squat emphasis + horizontal press/pull  
- **Tue:** Rest / easy walk + 5–10 min mobility (optional)  
- **Wed (2/25):** Full Body B — hinge emphasis + vertical pull + unilateral  
- **Thu:** Rest  
- **Fri (2/27):** Full Body C — hypertrophy + mobility/athletic emphasis  
- **Sat:** Rest / easy zone-2 cardio 20–30 min (optional)  
- **Sun:** Rest

---

## Key Metrics (Week Snapshot)
**Working-set volume (approx.)**
- **Mon:** ~20 working sets  
- **Wed:** ~18 working sets  
- **Fri:** ~20 working sets  
- **Total:** **~58 working sets**

**Baseline “main lift” working loads logged**
- **Goblet squat to box:** **60 lb × 8 × 3** @ RPE 6–7  
- **DB incline press:** **45s × 10 × 3** @ RPE 6–7  
- **Trap bar deadlift:** **185 lb × 5 × 4** @ RPE 6–7  
- **Leg press:** **270 lb × 12 × 3** @ RPE ~7  
- **Hip thrust:** **185 lb × 10 × 3** @ RPE ~7  
- **Back staples:** chest-supported row **90 × 10 × 3**; pulldown **120 × 10 × 2** (plus 110 × 10)

**Constraint management (tracked)**
- **Shoulder:** neutral-grip incline + machine press + landmine press; added cuff/scap work (face pulls + ER)  
- **Knee:** box target + controlled split squats + conservative leg press depth/stance

---

# Day 1 — Monday (Feb 23)  
### Full Body A (Squat + Incline Press + Row)
**Session target:** Smooth reps, conservative baseline (RPE 6–7)  
**Estimated duration:** ~75 min  
**Warm-up:** bike + shoulder prep (face pull/ER) + knee/hip prep (TKE/bridges/box squat)

### Main Work (working sets shown)
| Exercise | Working Sets | Notes |
|---|---:|---|
| **Goblet Squat to Box** | **60 × 8 × 3** | Controlled, soft touch to box; stop short of knee irritation |
| **DB Incline Press** (neutral grip) | **45s × 10 × 3** | Shoulder-friendly setup; avoid “pinchy” bottom range |
| **Chest-Supported Row (machine)** | **90 × 10 × 3** | 1-sec squeeze; no shrugging |
| **BB Romanian Deadlift** | **135 × 10 × 2** | Maintain lat tension; bar close |
| Cable Face Pull | **35 × 15 × 2** | Pull to forehead; no lumbar arch |
| Cable External Rotation | **5 × 12/side × 2** | Easy “grease the groove” |
| Pallof Press | **20 × 10/side × 2** | Exhale on press; anti-rotation focus |

**Day 1 set count:** ~**20 working sets**  
**Key takeaways:** Baseline loads established; pressing remains shoulder-managed via angle + grip.

---

# Day 2 — Wednesday (Feb 25)  
### Full Body B (Hinge + Vertical Pull + Unilateral + Carry)
**Session target:** Strong hinge work without next-day back fatigue  
**Estimated duration:** ~80 min  
**Warm-up:** rower + scap/shoulder prep + hip/knee prep + hinge drill

### Main Work (working sets shown)
| Exercise | Working Sets | Notes |
|---|---:|---|
| **Trap Bar Deadlift** (high handles) | **185 × 5 × 4** | Brace first; “push the floor away”; no grinding |
| **Neutral-Grip Lat Pulldown** | **110 × 10 × 1**, **120 × 10 × 2** | Stop short of shoulder discomfort |
| **Supported Split Squat (DB)** | **25s × 8/side × 2** | Control depth/stride for knee comfort |
| **Half-Kneeling Landmine Press** | **25 × 10/side × 3** | Press up/forward; ribs down |
| **Seated Leg Curl** | **70 × 12 × 1**, **80 × 12 × 2** | 1-sec squeeze; hips stay planted |
| **Farmer Carry (DB)** | **50s × 35s × 3** | Tall posture; stacked ribs/pelvis |

**Day 2 set count:** ~**18 working sets**  
**Key takeaways:** Trap bar work sat squarely in the “submax but productive” lane; unilateral work remained controlled for knee tolerance.

---

# Day 3 — Friday (Feb 27)  
### Full Body C (Hypertrophy + Joint-Friendly Pump + Mobility)
**Session target:** Quality volume, leave 1–2 reps in the tank  
**Estimated duration:** ~75 min  
**Warm-up:** bike + shoulder prep + knee/hip prep (TKE + patterning)

### Main Work (working sets shown)
| Exercise | Working Sets | Notes |
|---|---:|---|
| **Leg Press** (knee-friendly stance) | **270 × 12 × 3** | Adjust stance/depth to keep knee calm |
| **Seated Cable Row** | **110 × 12 × 1**, **120 × 12 × 2** | No torso swing; 1-count pause |
| **Machine Chest Press** | **70/side × 12 × 3** | Stop just shy of deepest stretch if shoulder “catches” |
| **Barbell Hip Thrust** | **185 × 10 × 3** | Ribs down, glutes lockout; 1-sec pause |
| Cable Lateral Raise | **10 × 15/side × 2** | Scapular plane; stop if pinch |
| Rear Delt Fly (machine) | **50 × 15 × 2** | Lead with elbows; no shrug |
| Standing Calf Raise | **120 × 12 × 2** | Full stretch + 1-sec squeeze |
| Dead Bug | **8/side × 2** | Exhale to pin ribs; low back stays down |

**Day 3 set count:** ~**20 working sets**  
**Key takeaways:** Hypertrophy day delivered good stimulus without joint irritation; shoulder-friendly pressing angle remained consistent.

---

## Weekly Notes & Considerations
- **Primary win:** Clear, repeatable baseline loads at **RPE 6–7** across the week (exactly the Phase 1 intent).
- **Shoulder management:** Pressing choices were appropriate (incline DB + machine press + landmine). Keep prioritizing longer warm-ups and avoid any sharp “pinch.”
- **Knee management:** Box target + supported split squats + conservative leg press depth worked well. Maintain controlled eccentrics and consistent stance.

---

## Progression Targets (Week 2 Suggestions — only if recovery/joints stayed normal)
Keep all work in the **RPE 6–7 lane**.
- **Trap bar deadlift:** add **+5–10 lb** (if all sets felt ≤7)  
- **Leg press:** add **+5–10 lb** *or* add **+1 rep/set** (keep depth knee-friendly)  
- **Hip thrust:** add **+5 lb total** *or* add **+1 rep/set**  
- **Presses/rows:** prefer **+1 rep per set** before load jumps unless reps are already at the top end with clean tempo

---
